key_insight: The paper couples sparsified physics-augmented neural constitutive models with FEM topology optimization to jointly optimize material composition and structure in multimaterial 3D printing end-to-end.
engineering_relevance: This enables interpretable, data-driven design of soft robotic and graded-material components while reducing reliance on trial-and-error prototyping.
method: Fit composition-aware hyperelastic laws from experimental data using sparsified physics-augmented neural networks.
Use closed-form symbolic constitutive expressions to enable exact adjoint differentiation in FEniCSx.
Perform concurrent optimization of topology and spatial material distribution under stretch/non-failure constraints.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deployment on soft robotic gripper cases, including continuous composition optimization for anisotropic contact behavior and joint topology-material optimization.
watch_item: The workflow depends on high-quality experimental training data and careful integration of learned constitutive laws with adjoint/FEM solvers to preserve stability and differentiability.
paper_title: Towards end-to-end optimization in multimaterial 3D printing
